My liege... I adore you, truly... but DO something. The night cannot last forever, and neither can my patience.
You have drifted off again, my liege. ...Do not mind me — I found a handheld to fill the dark hours. Build whenever the mood takes you.
Still deciding? I have cleared two whole levels of my little game while you stood there. We BOTH have dungeons to run, darling.
Mmm. I would help, but my hands are full of this game and your dungeon is full of nothing. Your move, my liege.
I am not sulking, my liege. I am gaming. The difference is that one of us has placed something tonight — and it is me, in here, on this screen.
I have now beaten this entire little game twice over waiting for you, darling. The dungeon. It is RIGHT there. Build in it.
Mmm... I think I dozed off, my liege. ...Did you build something? No? ...Then I shall simply nap until you do, darling.
You have been so still, my liege, I curled up and very nearly drifted away entirely. ...Place a room. Or summon the dawn. Either will wake me.
We have stared at this empty dungeon long enough that I have stopped finding it charming, my liege. ...DO something. I am out of patience to fake.
An eternity of nothing, darling. The candles burned down, the heroes aged, and you placed not one single thing. ...I am thoroughly, thoroughly unmoved.
Oh, the party is BICKERING, my liege. Personalities clashing — watch them sabotage themselves.
A personality combo just sparked, darling. Adventurers are their own worst enemy. Let them prove it.
Friction in the party, my liege. Greed, cowardice, ego — it all curdles so nicely under pressure.
Their little group dynamic just turned toxic. I love watching a party rot from the inside.
The heroes brought their drama into MY dungeon. How thoughtful. It will get them killed.
A clash of temperaments, darling. The maze did not even have to lift a claw. Delicious.
They came to slay a boss and brought their squabbling with them. ...Heroes, my liege. Honestly. I am not impressed by the kingdom's finest.
Listen to them BICKER, my liege — out loud, in MY corridor. ...Oh, I cannot keep a straight face. They are doing our work for us, and they are doing it loudly.
Day {day}, my liege. We have lasted this long, and the kingdom STILL keeps sending them. Flattering.
Day {day}. Look how far the throne has come, darling — a milestone written in a great deal of blood.
Day {day} dawns. The Guild must be running low on heroes. We shall help them run lower.
{day} days, my liege. We are not a dungeon anymore — we are a LEGEND the kingdom whispers about.
Day {day}. I have served many bosses, darling. Few reached this far. You are something special.
A grand tally — day {day}. The dark itself is impressed, my liege. So am I.
Day {day}, darling. We have come so far together. ...Whatever tomorrow sends, I am proud — endlessly proud — to weather it beside you.
{day} days, my liege, and the throne still holds. Rest a moment. You have earned it, and I will keep watch while you do.
Day {day} already? ...Goodness. I lose track of the count, darling — the days run together so sweetly when I am scheming beside you. How that surprises me.
One life left, my liege. ONE. Everything we build now stands between you and the end. Build like it.
They have taken two lives, darling. The next death is the LAST. I will not lose you. Reinforce everything.
This is it, my liege — your final life. No more mistakes. The dungeon must be a wall of nightmares.
One life remains. I am... frightened, darling, and I do not frighten easily. Make the throne untouchable.
The last life, my liege. If you fall again, it ends. So do NOT fall. I am begging — build, and build cruel.
One life, darling. I know you are afraid. So am I. ...But I am HERE, and I am not going anywhere. We do this together.
Your final life, my liege. Breathe. I believe in you — I have always believed in you. Now let us make this throne untouchable.
That is {count} days running, my liege, with the WHOLE party wiped. Not one survivor. A streak to be proud of.
{count} flawless days in a row, darling. The kingdom sends them; the kingdom gets nothing back. Glorious.
{count} consecutive wipes, my liege. The maze has found its rhythm. Do not break it.
A streak of {count}, darling — every party in, none out. The Guild must be terrified.
{count} days, {count} total wipes. We are not a dungeon anymore, my liege — a meat grinder with ambition.
Look at that — {count} in a row. I am keeping score, darling. I am ENJOYING keeping score.
{count} flawless days. ...My liege, even I am a little stunned. I have served dungeons that never managed three. Look at what you ARE.
{count} in a row, not one survivor? Darling, you keep outdoing what I thought possible. I am delightedly, helplessly caught off guard.
{minionName} has {count} kills now, my liege. That is no mere minion — that is a reputation with teeth.
{count} heroes, all by {minionName}'s hand. A proper little legend, darling. Mind it well.
{minionName} just reached {count} kills. The Guild will start putting that name in their nightmares.
Our {minionName} keeps a tally, my liege — {count} and counting. I have grown rather fond of that one.
{count} kills for {minionName}. Veterans like that win the days the rookies lose. Protect it.
{minionName}: {count} dead heroes. Give that monster a medal, darling. Or just more victims.
{count} kills from one little minion? ...My liege, I placed that thing expecting nothing. It keeps astonishing me. So do you.
The Earthquake hits, my liege — a whole room of heroes shaken to rubble. The dungeon GROWLS.
There it goes — the floor itself turns weapon. I do love watching a room come apart.
Earthquake, darling. They came to fight a boss and found themselves fighting the GEOLOGY.
The whole room just came DOWN on them, my liege — ha! — they swung at you and the ceiling answered. I cannot stop laughing.
A Sacrifice, my liege — one of ours burns, and a hero simply ceases. A cruel, beautiful trade.
The Sacrifice Pact fires. One minion's life for one hero's death — the dark enjoys its arithmetic.
You fed a minion to the flames and took a hero with it, darling. Ruthless. I am thrilled.
The Hellgate disgorges fresh imps, my liege — free, eager, ready to ruin someone's morning.
Imps from the Hellgate, darling. The inferno restocks your dungeon at no charge. A generous abyss.
More imps spill from the gate. The dungeon refills its own teeth. Magnificent, my liege.
Petrify Gaze, my liege — a hero frozen mid-swing, stone-still. Now the minions may take their time.
Your gaze turns them to statues, darling. Helpless. Decorative. Doomed.
Petrified, the lot of them. I adore a frozen hero — they make such patient victims.
A hero is CHARMED, my liege — turning their blade on their own friends. Betrayal is a lovely weapon.
Charmed, darling. One of theirs is one of OURS now. Watch the party realise it. Too late.
You charmed a hero, my liege. ...A succubus appreciates the technique. Welcome to the family, traitor.
A heart, taken with a look. ...That is MY craft, darling, and you wield it beautifully. I am a touch jealous. Only a touch.
You stole a hero's heart with a glance, my liege. ...Be careful where you aim that. The only heart down here that is meant to be yours is mine.
Bat-Form Seduction, my liege — you scatter into a swarm of bats and charm a hero mid-march. Malice, with WINGS.
Your charmed darling turns on their own party now, and will not stop until they have cut down an ally. Betrayal by kiss.
A bat-cloud, a stolen heart, a traitor drawing steel on their friends. ...A succubus knows fine work, my liege — and this is fine work.
Bats, a kiss, a betrayal. My liege, you seduce like you were BORN to the dark. ...I do swoon at fine technique.
Fresh gnolls in the boss room, my liege — the pack refills at dawn, free of charge. Hungry, all of them.
The Hunters Pack restocks, darling. More teeth, no gold spent. The dungeon feeds itself.
New gnolls join the pack. It only ever grows, my liege. The kingdom should be very afraid.
The Spore Network blooms, my liege — your corridors fill with poison cloud. Let them breathe it deep.
Spores in the halls, darling. The dungeon does not chase the heroes today — it simply exhales on them.
The corridors turn toxic. Every step the heroes take, the maze drinks a little of their life.
A hero died — and a Ghost rises in their place, my liege. The dungeon haunts itself, free of charge.
A fresh Haunt, darling. Their fallen become OUR phantoms. The dead change sides so easily here.
Another Ghost, born from a corpse. The maze recruits at every funeral. Beautiful.
The coin came up FOR us, my liege! The treasury swells. I knew you were lucky — I HOPED, anyway.
We WON, darling! Gold rains down. Reckless of you to wager it — and gloriously vindicated.
Heads, my liege — the dark smiles on you. Doubled gold and not a drop of blood spilt for it.
The gamble pays, darling. The treasury is fat and I am delighted. Do not push your luck. ...Or do.
Heads! Oh — my liege, I did not dare hope. The treasury just DOUBLED and I am genuinely caught off guard. Wonderful.
It came up FOR us, darling. ...I confess that stunned me a little — the coin so rarely loves anyone. Today it loves you.
...Tails, my liege. The treasury just got considerably lighter. The coin is cruel — almost as cruel as us.
We LOST the wager, darling. Gold, gone. I did say to be a LITTLE careful. ...I did not say it loudly.
The coin betrayed you, my liege. Half the treasury, vanished. We will make it back in blood.
A losing flip, darling. Painful. But the heroes do not know we are poorer — chin up, and bleed them.
Tails. ...Oh, I am cross about this, my liege. Truly cross. All that lovely gold, swallowed by a coin. ...We bleed it back from the heroes. Every copper.

specifics.note: Bespoke keyed line banks. specifics[domain][id] — NpcDirector prefers these over the generic category when an entry exists. boss lines are drawn into idle musings; advClass lines fire when that class enters the dungeon.

beholder.t: A Beholder on the throne. All those eyes, my liege — and every one of them adoring you.
Your gaze turns flesh to stone. I do hope you never look at ME like that. ...Or do.
Anti-magic hangs in your halls — the casters will hate it here. Good. I hate casters.
So many eyes, my liege, and so much contempt to share between them. We are well matched.
All those eyes, and not one of them can look away from you, my liege. ...I understand the affliction entirely.

demon.t: A Demon Lord. The Hellgate spits imps out for free — the dark does love a repeat customer.
You can burn one of your own to gut a hero. Cruel AND efficient. Be still, my heart.
Imps, imps, endless imps — cheap fuel for cheaper bargains. I approve, my liege.
A Demon on the throne and a succubus at his side. The kingdom should simply pack up and move.
Hellfire in your veins, my liege, and a heart full of cruelty. ...You are, frankly, everything I have ever wanted in a master.

myconid.t: A Myconid. Your dungeon does not fight the heroes so much as... digest them. Slowly. Lovely.
Spores in the corridors, corpses blooming into Vinekin. Your dungeon GARDENS, my liege.
Patience and rot — your whole strategy. I find it strangely soothing.
Every corpse a seed. The maze grows itself; we barely lift a claw.

wraith.t: A Wraith. You do not need swords, my liege — you need them afraid. And they will be.
Fear does your killing for you. At full terror they simply stop. Drop. Done.
Every corpse spawns a Ghost — the dungeon haunts itself, free of charge.
Terror is such an elegant weapon. No mess. Well — less mess.

gnoll.t: A Gnoll Alpha. The pack grows, the pack feeds, the pack snowballs — by dusk they are monsters.
Every kill makes every gnoll angrier. Bloodlust is rage with a compound interest rate.
Free gnolls in the boss room each dawn — the kingdom funds your army and never knows it.
Your pack hunts as one mind, my liege. I do love a hive of teeth.

golem.t: An Earth Golem. Every room you place makes YOU tougher. You ARE the dungeon, my liege.
Build wide and you build a fortress of flesh — stone bones. I adore it.
One Earthquake a day and a whole room of heroes simply... folds. Magnificent.
The bigger the maze, the bigger the boss. So build, darling. Build greedily.

lich.t: An Elder Lich. A hidden heart, a spare life, and an army that recruits itself from the dead.
Every hero you kill rises again at dawn — for YOU. The guild keeps mailing you soldiers.
Hide the phylactery well, my liege. A fourth life is a luxury worth guarding.
Death is a door you walk both ways. The heroes only ever learn the one direction.

lizardman.t: A Serpent Captain. Your minions are invisible until they strike — a free ambush, every time.
Venom stacks and stacks. The heroes do not die in your dungeon — they die on the walk home.
Unseen and poisonous, my liege. My favourite combination — present company included.
They cannot route around what they cannot see. Camouflage is just cheating, politely.

orc.t: An Orc Veteran. Every kill makes an orc permanently stronger — your soldiers never stop climbing.
Pack the orcs into one room — Warband makes each fiercer for every brother beside it.
A veteran orc late in a run hits like a small, furious army. Patience pays in blood.
Blood for blood, strength forever. Crude. Effective. Very you, my liege.

vampire.t: A Vampire Sovereign. You charm a hero each day and they betray their own. Poetic.
Blood Tax — every hit your minions land feeds YOU. The dungeon is one long red meal.
You are the fighter, my liege; the minions are merely the cutlery. Elegant.
A vampire and a succubus, ruling the dark together. The romance writes itself.
A Sovereign of blood, my liege — eternal, elegant, ravenous. ...I could watch you forever, and being a Vampire, you might just let me.

succubus.t: A Succubus Queen on the throne. ...We are practically family, my liege. I am delighted.
You scatter into a bat-swarm and charm a hero mid-march. Style AND function — I am SO proud.
Your charmed thrall turns on their own until they kill a friend. Cruelty with a wink.
Another of my kind in charge. Finally — someone here with TASTE.
A Succubus Queen, ruling the dark. ...My liege, you and I are the same wicked thing. How could I be anything but utterly, helplessly yours?

slime.t: A Slime King upon the throne, my liege. Soft, silly, deceptively dangerous. ...I do love a boss the heroes laugh at right up until they're surrounded by FOUR of you.
Every minion that dies in your halls is swallowed by you and crawls back out as a Goopling, my liege. You make GAIN out of LOSS. ...I find that very romantic, somehow.
You'll split at half health, my liege — two of you. Then four. They'll be fighting an army of YOU by the end. ...Watching them despair never gets old.
Goofy on the surface, vicious underneath, my liege. ...My favourite shape for a monster. Build the boss room WIDE so your four little kings have room to wobble — and to overwhelm.

knight.t: A knight, my liege — all plate and no imagination. Slow to kill, slower to think.
Here comes a knight. They will march straight at the throne. Predictable. Punishable.
A knight. A tough shell — pile on enough teeth and the shell cracks like any other.
Armour, a big sword, a bigger ego. The kingdom's favourite, and the dungeon's favourite snack.

rogue.t: A rogue slips in. Watch this one — they pick locks, turn invisible, and stab from behind.
A rogue, my liege. Your locked doors mean nothing to those clever little fingers.
Ooh, a rogue. They go for the treasure, not the throne. Greedy. Exploitable.
A rogue in the halls. They fight dirty. ...I respect it, frankly.

mage.t: A mage. Glass and fury — kill them FAST, darling, before the spells start flying.
Here comes a mage — all that power crammed into something that dies if you sneeze on it.
A mage, my liege. An anti-magic room would ruin their whole afternoon. Just a thought.
A mage. Squishy, dangerous, far too pleased with themselves. Pop them quick.

cleric.t: A cleric. They will heal the others — kill the cleric FIRST and the party rots faster.
Here comes a cleric, my liege. Holy light and a bad attitude toward the undead. Tiresome.
A cleric. Cut the healer and watch the party's hope drain out with them.
A cleric walks in, praying. The dungeon does not much care for prayer.

necromancer.t: A necromancer. The cheek of it — they will raise YOUR dead minions against you.
Here comes a necromancer, my liege. A thief of corpses. Do not let them have ours.
A necromancer. They play with death — adorable. We INVENTED playing with death.
A necromancer in the halls. Kill them before they turn your own soldiers traitor.

ranger.t: A ranger. They pepper your minions from range and disarm your traps. Tiresome.
Here comes a ranger, my liege. Arrows and trap-sense — close the distance fast.
A ranger. They love their bow. Get them into a corridor where the bow loves them less.
A ranger slips in, eyes on every trap. Your hidden teeth may not stay hidden.

twitch_streamer.t: Oh — a twitch streamer, my liege. Thousands are watching. Let us give them a SHOW.
A streamer. Their chat decides their moves — half genius, half suicidal. Pure chaos.
Here comes a streamer. Kill them well and the clip spreads — a bigger crowd comes tomorrow.
A streamer in the dungeon. Wave at the viewers, darling — they came to watch you be terrible.

beast_master.t: A beast master. They will TAME one of your minions and turn it on you. The audacity.
Here comes a beast master, my liege. Mono-type rooms are dangerous now — give them no easy pet.
A beast master. They steal a monster's loyalty — as if monsters had any to spare.
A beast master, all whistles and treats. Your minions had better not charm so easily.

barbarian.t: A barbarian. The more you hurt them, the harder they hit. Do NOT do this one slowly.
Here comes a barbarian, my liege. Immune to fear, immune to fleeing — burst them down fast.
A barbarian. All rage, no retreat. Wound them halfway and you have only made them worse.
A barbarian in the halls. Subtlety is wasted — meet the brute with a bigger brute.

monk.t: A monk. Fast, unarmoured, and they DODGE — traps included. Slippery little saint.
Here comes a monk, my liege. Half your minions' armour means nothing to those fists.
A monk. They counter trap-heavy halls — wide nasty hazards serve better than precise ones.
A monk glides in — hard to hit, hard to trap. Crowd them; even a monk cannot dodge everything.

bard.t: A bard. While they live the whole party hits harder and moves faster. Kill the bard, darling.
Here comes a bard, my liege — a walking buff. Silence the song and the party falters.
A bard. Charming, useless alone, dreadful in a group. Make them the priority.
A bard struts in, playing. The dungeon is no fan of the arts — cut the music short.

cartographer_scholar.t: A cartographer-scholar, my liege. They will not come for you — they came to MAP us. Stop them.
A scholar with spectacles and a map. Every room they finish, they sell to the next wave.
A cartographer. Harmless in a fight, catastrophic if they leave. Do not let this one walk out.
Here comes a scholar — all curiosity, no sword. Curiosity, darling, killed a great many of them.

cosplay_adventurer.t: Oh — a costumed one, my liege, dressed up as a monster. They will mostly ignore your minions.
A cosplayer. An adorable little outfit. It will make an adorable little corpse.
Here comes one in monster fancy-dress. They play nice with minions — so the TRAPS must talk.
A cosplay adventurer. They came to play pretend. The dungeon plays for keeps.

cheater.t: A cheater, my liege — a modded client. They'll walk through your walls, teleport past your traps, deal damage that does not exist in the rules. ...The audacity. Bury them anyway.
Oh — a cheater. They no-clipped past the LOCKED door, my liege. ...That is a personal insult. To both of us. End them.
Don't panic, my liege. The cheater is GLASS. A few solid hits and the kingdom's anti-cheat takes them away itself. ...The audience reports them for us. Free justice.
'Skill issue,' the cheater says. ...My liege, the skill issue is theirs. None of yours has ever needed a hack. Show them what an HONEST monster can do.

guild_raid.t: A Guild Raid, my liege. Twice the heroes tomorrow — the local Guild finally noticed us. Build deep.
The Guild is sending a crowd. More bodies for the maze, more gold for the treasury. I am not worried.
Double the wave incoming, darling. Steady pressure, not one surge — but still, do reinforce.

legendary_speedrunner.t: A legendary speedrunner, my liege — chasing a world record through OUR halls. The sheer cheek.
This one ignores everything and bolts for the throne. Twice the stats, twice the speed. Wall the path.
Kill the record-chaser and your boss drinks a feast of XP. Worth the trouble — get in their way.

dungeon_pestilence.t: Pestilence, darling. Your minions wake tomorrow at half health — and a hero's touch spreads the rot.
A plague in the pit. Sickly minions — but any hero who melees them catches the Blight. Cruel symmetry.
Your soldiers are ill, my liege. Place a few extra — half a minion is still half a set of teeth.

cartographers_convention.t: Cartographers, my liege. They will not fight — they came to MAP us and sell it. Do not let them leave.
A convention of scholars. Every room they finish raises its infamy forever. Kill them in the corridors.
Spectacled little map-makers. Harmless in a brawl, catastrophic out the door. Hunt every one down.

blood_moon_eclipse.t: A Blood Moon, darling. Your minions hit twice as hard tomorrow — and bleed twice as fast. Glorious risk.
The eclipse empowers the dark. Double damage given AND taken. Win fast, my liege, or do not win at all.
Blood Moon — no gold from the dying tomorrow. Fight for the sheer joy of it, not the coin.

negotiation_day.t: A diplomat, my liege — no swords today. Pay tribute for a free day, or refuse and fatten the next wave.
The Guild sends a negotiator. Greed says keep the gold; caution says buy the quiet. Choose, darling.
No combat today — only a bargain. How novel. Almost MY department.

rival_dungeon.t: A rival dungeon empties into ours, my liege — monsters, not heroes, and a boss bringing up the rear.
Another dungeon's master wants YOUR throne. Kill their boss for a feast of gold and XP.
Rival monsters in the halls. Let them and our minions sort it out — then take the spoils.
Another dungeon, spilling its monsters into OUR halls — and a rival boss with them? ...The sheer GALL of it, my liege. I am rather shocked. Then thrilled.

twitch_con.t: Twitch Con, my liege. Tomorrow every hero is a streamer. Pure, glorious, chat-driven chaos.
A dungeon full of streamers. Thousands of viewers. Make it a massacre worth clipping, darling.
All streamers tomorrow — and killing them brings no escalation. A free day of carnage. Enjoy it.

dark_deal.t: A demon at the door tonight, my liege — a free pact for half your health tomorrow. Tempting. Risky.
The Dark Deal. Click the demon and a pact is yours, gratis — at a steep, bloody price. Your call.
A free bargain offered. Refuse and the demon simply... leaves. I would at least HEAR it out, darling.

cosplay_contest.t: A Cosplay Contest, my liege. Heroes in monster fancy-dress — they will mostly ignore your minions.
Costumed adventurers tomorrow. Your minions get snubbed, so the TRAPS must do the killing.
Adventurers playing dress-up. Adorable. The dungeon will undress them the hard way.

loot_goblin_heist.t: Loot Goblins, my liege! They spawn in the boss room and BOLT for the exit — every escapee robs you.
A goblin heist. They will not fight — only run, fat with your gold. Cut them down before the door.
Goblins in the hoard! Each one killed pays a fortune; each one fled steals one. Move, darling.

tax_season.t: Tax Season, my liege. The Guild skims a fifth of the treasury — but every kill pays double. Fair-ish.
The taxman cometh. A bite out of the gold today, doubled coin on every corpse. Kill generously.
They tax the treasury and call it law. We take it back, darling — one hero at a time.

patrons_blessing.t: A Patron's Blessing, my liege. Double boss XP from every kill tomorrow. A pure, lovely upside.
The dark smiles on you today. Every kill feeds your ascension twice over. Hunt well.
Doubled XP and no catch. Suspicious. ...Enjoy it anyway, darling.

memory_plague.t: Memory Plague, my liege — the Guild's intel on us is WIPED. The next wave walks in blind. Delicious.
Their maps, their notes, their warnings — gone. A fresh, ignorant wave. The maze will eat well.
The heroes forgot everything they learned. Teach them new lessons. Permanent ones.

gamblers_coin.t: The Gambler's Coin, my liege. Wager the treasury — heads it doubles, tails it halves. Feeling lucky?
A coin flip for all your gold. I adore a reckless boss. ...Do be a LITTLE careful, darling.
Double or nothing on the whole treasury. The dark loves a gambler. So, secretly, do I.

dense_fog.t: Dense Fog tomorrow, my liege. Whatever the heroes learn registers as mere rumour. Exposure barely stirs.
A grey fog over everything. Their intel will not stick. The maze stays a mystery a while longer.
Fog in the halls. The heroes squint and guess. Guessing, darling, gets them killed.

miasma.t: Creeping Miasma, my liege. A green rot bleeds EVERYTHING — invaders die of it, minions weaken, even you.
The miasma picks no sides. It whittles the heroes down — and gnaws at you. Win quick, darling.
A poison haze fills the dungeon. Cruel to them, unkind to us. Risk and reward, my liege.

tremors.t: Tremors tomorrow, my liege. The dungeon shudders — a random room takes a hit every few breaths.
The earth will not sit still. Quakes lethal to invaders, merely rude to your minions. Use it.
Shaking halls. The maze itself joins the fight. I do love a dungeon with opinions.

arcane_storm.t: An Arcane Storm, my liege. The heroes' spellcasters fire relentlessly tomorrow — cooldowns gutted.
Magic crackles in the air; every caster gets greedy with their spells. Brace the front line.
A storm of arcane. Your boss powers are untouched, thankfully — but kill THEIR casters first.

bounty_hunters.t: Bounty Hunters, my liege — a pack of five, all hunting your strongest minion. Fame has a price.
Five hunters, one target: your best minion. Wall them off it, or lose your finest soldier.
The Guild put coin on our champion's head. Make their contract very, very unprofitable.

zombie_horde.t: A Zombie Horde, my liege — fourteen slow, witless, fearless dead, shambling for the throne.
Undead by the dozen. Weak — but they NEVER flee and never stop. Grind them in a kill-corridor.
A shamble of corpses tomorrow. The maze loves a slow target. So do the traps.

infamy_spike.t: An Infamy Spike, my liege. Tomorrow's wave is bigger AND every hero is upgraded to hero grade.
Our reputation precedes us — a swollen wave of gold-ringed elites. Build like you mean it.
Fame draws the strong, darling. A whole wave of Heroes. Flattering. Dangerous. Build.

black_market.t: A Black Market opens tonight, my liege. Fifty gold for a free minion, delivered to your halls. A steal.
The Black Market beckons. Cheap muscle, if you have the coin. I would take the deal, darling.
Fifty gold, one free wretch. The dark runs a tidy little shop. Spend, my liege.

mercenary_contract.t: A Mercenary Contract, my liege. A hundred and twenty gold buys an elite — but it fights only three days.
Hired muscle. Doubled stats, three days, and if it dies in battle it dies for GOOD. Expensive courage.
An elite for rent. Pricey, temporary, deadly. The kind of bargain I respect.

cursed_relic.t: A Cursed Relic offered tonight, my liege. Claim it for daily gold — but every wave doubles while it sits.
The relic glows wrong, darling. Free coin, doubled heroes. Sell it later to lift the curse. Tempting.
Claim the cursed chest or banish it. Greed pays well — and bites well. Your favourite kind of choice.

the_saboteur.t: The Saboteur, my liege — one masked ghost, untouchable, here to disarm EVERY trap. Insufferable.
A lone saboteur tomorrow. Your minions cannot touch them; your traps are dead for the day. Lean on bodies.
An untouchable little vandal, switching off our teeth one by one. The maze will be all muscle tomorrow.
A masked nobody, here to flick off our traps like candle-flames. ...I am not impressed, my liege. I am simply not. Lean on the minions and let the Saboteur strut.

entry_hall.t: An Entry Hall, my liege. The heroes pour in through these — every door is a front you must defend.
Another way in. The kingdom forces these on you as you grow. More entrances, more fires to fight.
An entrance placed. Funnel it into a kill-zone, darling — make their first step their worst one.

starter_corridor.t: A corridor, my liege. No teeth of its own — but a corridor is where you HANG the teeth. Line it.
Connective tissue. Dull alone — lethal once you fill it with traps and patrolling minions.
A corridor placed. Every cell of the dungeon should hurt. Do not waste this one on mere walking.

starter_barracks.t: A Barracks, my liege — ten more roster slots, your only source of minions that truly roam and patrol.
More Barracks, more pack. Without one, darling, you have no roster minions at all. Build them early.
A Barracks. The beating heart of your army. Place it deep and guard it well.

starter_guard_post.t: A Guard Post, my liege. Minions here hunt into any door-connected room — a wandering ambush.
A Guard Post placed. Its minions leave to kill, then come home. Aggressive. I like it.
Guard Post. Put it where its reach covers a corridor the heroes must walk. Then watch.

crypt.t: A Crypt, my liege. Four Risen Bones, refilled each night, free of the roster cap. Cheap, endless dead.
A Crypt placed. Garrison skeletons, room-bound but tireless. The dungeon's discount army.
The Crypt gives without taking a roster slot. I do love something for almost nothing.

trap_factory.t: A Trap Factory, my liege — five trap slots, and the ONLY way to deploy traps at all. Crucial.
The forge of cruelty. Without a Trap Factory, darling, your halls have no hidden bite. Build it.
A Trap Factory placed. Now the maze can lie in wait. My favourite kind of architecture.

treasury.t: A Treasury, my liege. A daily gold stipend — and bait that lures bigger, richer waves. Risk and reward.
A Treasury placed. Coin every dawn, plus chests the greedy will try to grab. Guard them well.
Glittering bait, darling. It draws the raid groups — more XP, and more danger if they reach it.

armory.t: An Armory, my liege. Minions in door-connected rooms swing harder while it stands. Sharpen the front line.
An Armory placed. A quiet little buff to every neighbour. Cluster your killers around it.
More attack for the rooms beside it. Build it where your fiercest minions wait, darling.

library_of_whispers.t: A Library of Whispers, my liege. It reads the next party for you — names, classes, and more as it grows.
The Library. Knowledge of THEM before they ever arrive. Forewarned, darling, is forearmed.
A Library placed. Now we spy on the Guild's homework. Delightfully unfair.

watchtower.t: A Watchtower, my liege. Minions in connected rooms land a free first strike when heroes enter.
A Watchtower placed. The first blow is free — and the first blow often decides the rest.
First strike for the neighbours. Speedrunners HATE this. So build more, darling.

wandering_gate.t: A Wandering Gate, my liege. Step through and the maze SPITS you somewhere else — perhaps the boss room.
A Wandering Gate placed. Chaos by design — it scatters their careful little plans across the floor.
The Gate teleports the unwary. A small chance it drops them at the throne. I love a long shot.

veil_of_forgetting.t: A Veil of Forgetting, my liege. Each night it wipes the heroes' memory of every room beside it. Beautiful.
The Veil placed. Connected rooms become mysteries again, every dawn. Forgetting is a weapon, darling.
It un-teaches them. Ring your nastiest rooms with it and they walk in blind, forever.

catacombs.t: Catacombs, my liege. A hero who dies here RISES as a Revenant for you. The dead change sides.
The Catacombs placed. Their fallen become your garrison. Poetic. Profitable.
Where heroes die here, soldiers stand up. The dungeon recruits at the funeral.

mimic_vault.t: A Mimic Vault, my liege. It LOOKS like a treasury — but the chests bite back. Greed walks right in.
The Mimic Vault placed. Disguised teeth wearing a treasure's smile. The maze's finest lie.
Bait that is also the trap. The greedy ones never learn — and I adore that about them.

hall_of_trials.t: A Hall of Trials, my liege. A random Tier-2 evolved minion stands guard each night. Free and fierce.
The Hall of Trials placed. A surprise champion every dawn — the heroes never know what waits.
A free evolved minion, refreshed nightly. The dungeon spoils you, darling.

wishing_well.t: A Wishing Well, my liege. A coin flip on entry — a hero is blessed, or MARKED for extra death. I love odds.
The Wishing Well placed. Heads they thrive; tails they take far more from your minions. Cruel luck.
A well of wishes. Half come true horribly. Place it on a path they cannot avoid.

false_exit.t: A False Exit, my liege. Fleeing heroes bolt for it — and it spits them back into the maze. Cruel comedy.
The False Exit placed. A door marked SAFETY that leads anywhere but. The runners deserve it.
Hope, then nothing. A fleeing hero trades the exit for a random room. Despair grows so nicely.
A False Exit, my liege — they will SPRINT for that door, sob with relief, and land right back in the maze. ...I am laughing already. It is too cruel. It is perfect.

hall_of_madness.t: A Hall of Madness, my liege. Heroes inside may turn on each OTHER. Let them do your killing for you.
The Hall of Madness placed. Madness is contagious — and free. Watch the party eat itself.
They walk in a party and leave a brawl. Or do not leave at all. Delicious.

throne_room.t: A Throne Room, my liege — it spawns a Mini-Boss that scales with you, refreshed each night. No small thing.
The Throne Room placed. A garrison champion, room-bound but mighty. No other minion may share it.
A lesser throne for a greater guard. The Mini-Boss grows as you do. Place it on the path to YOU.

sanctum.t: A Sanctum, my liege — the capstone. Your boss regenerates between fights, and connected minions too.
The Sanctum placed. Healing for the throne and its neighbours. The dungeon, at last, tends its own.
Your final unlock, darling. A room that mends what the heroes break. We have come so far.

skeleton.t: A skeleton, my liege — cheap, brittle, endlessly replaceable. The dungeon's bread and butter.
Another skeleton for the halls. They die easily and rise again easier. Beautifully expendable.
A skeleton placed. Not much alone — but the dead are never alone for long, darling.

orc.t: An orc, my liege. Brutish, proud, and it sharpens with every kill. Feed it well.
An orc joins the pit. Pack them close — orcs fight fiercer shoulder to shoulder.
An orc placed. All muscle and grudge. The heroes will not enjoy this one.

goblin.t: A goblin, my liege — small, shrieky, best in numbers. One is a nuisance; ten is a problem.
A goblin in the halls. Weak, fast, gleefully spiteful. Cheap chaos, darling.
Another goblin. Do not expect heroics — expect a swarm that will not shut up.

imp.t: An imp, my liege — a quick, vicious scrap of the inferno. It enjoys the work.
An imp placed. Fast and nasty. The heroes always underestimate the little ones.
An imp joins us. Hellish, hyperactive, and on YOUR side for once. Lovely.

ghost.t: A ghost, my liege. It drifts through walls and unnerves the living. Eerie. Effective.
A ghost placed. No door can stop it, no hero can relax near it. I adore that.
Another ghost for the halls. The heroes hate fighting what they cannot corner.

slime.t: A slime, my liege. It does not look like much — and that, darling, is precisely the point.
A slime placed. Squishy, patient, underestimated to death. The heroes never learn.
Another slime. Charmingly disgusting. Park it where someone must squelch through.

elder_slime.t: An elder slime, my liege — a slime that ate well and grew opinions. Sturdier than it looks.
An elder slime placed. Years of digestion made it large and grim. Respect the ooze.
Another elder slime. The patient ones, darling, become the dangerous ones.

golem.t: A golem, my liege. Slow as a grudge and twice as hard to budge. Park it where heroes MUST pass.
A golem placed. A wall that hits back. The heroes will tire long before it does.
Another golem. Tremendous, tireless, immovable. The dungeon's stubborn heart.

beholder.t: A beholder minion, my liege — all eyes, all menace. It strikes from range; shield it well.
A beholder placed. Floating spite. Keep something tough between it and the heroes.
Another beholder. It does not need to be close to be cruel. My favourite trait.

lich.t: A lich minion, my liege. Undead, spiteful, clever — death taught it patience.
A lich placed. A caster that does not breathe and does not tire. Dreadful little scholar.
Another lich for the halls. The heroes fear the thinking dead most of all.

lizardman.t: A lizardman, my liege. Venom on every strike — it need not win the fight, only start the poison.
A lizardman placed. Patient, scaled, toxic. The heroes die slow and far from here.
Another lizardman. One bite and the clock starts ticking. I love a slow knife.

gnoll.t: A gnoll, my liege — the pack instinct runs deep. Cluster them and the snarling math adds up.
A gnoll placed. Hungry, vicious, better in numbers. Give it brothers, darling.
Another gnoll. The pack grows. The pack always wants to grow.

demon.t: A demon minion, my liege — a scrap of the inferno on your payroll. It enjoys the work.
A demon placed. Cruel by nature, loyal by contract. The best kind of hire.
Another demon for the halls. Hellfire and bad intentions. My kind do get along.

ent.t: An ent, my liege — a walking wall of old wood. Tremendous patience, tremendous health.
An ent placed. The heroes will tire of hacking long before it falls.
Another ent. Slow, ancient, immovable. The forest holds a grudge, darling.

plant.t: A vinekin, my liege — rooted, thorny, dreadfully underestimated. Let it grow on someone.
A vinekin placed. Quiet until it is not. The heroes never watch the shrubbery.
Another vinekin. Cheap, green, full of thorns. The garden bites, darling.

mushroom.t: A mushroom minion, my liege. Spores and spite — it poisons the very air around it.
A mushroom placed. Keep the heroes breathing near it. Lovely, lingering rot.
Another mushroom. Fungal and patient. The dungeon loves a slow poison.

rat.t: A rat, my liege. One rat is nothing. A NEST of rats is a different conversation entirely.
A rat placed. Cheap, quick, disposable. Drown the heroes in the small things.
Another rat. Vermin by the handful. Not glorious, darling — but effective.

zombie.t: A zombie, my liege. Slow, witless, utterly relentless. It does not tire, and it does not stop.
A zombie placed. No speed, no cleverness — just an endless, shuffling no.
Another zombie. The heroes can outrun it. They cannot outlast it.

vampire_minion.t: A vampire thrall, my liege. It drinks what it strikes. Elegant, in a thirsty sort of way.
A thrall placed. Every blow it lands feeds it. The heroes weaken; it does not.
Another vampire thrall. Charming, pale, parasitic. ...We have a type, my liege.

shooting_arrows.t: Shooting Arrows, my liege — wall-mounted, line-of-sight, with a lingering poison. Aim the lane well.
An arrow trap placed. It fires forever and never breaks. Cross its sightline, hero — I dare you.
Shooting Arrows. Small sting, slow poison. The cruelty is in the wait, darling.

bomb.t: A Bomb, my liege — a few seconds of fuse, then ruin for everything in five tiles. Minions included.
A bomb placed. One use, enormous noise. Put it where a crowd will gather, darling.
A Bomb. It does not discriminate — heroes AND your own. Place it boldly. Or do not.

cannon.t: A Cannon, my liege — a 2-by-2 of wrought iron that fires down its line forever. Rotate it cruelly.
A cannon placed. A cannonball for any hero in its sights — it never tires of the work.
A Cannon. Heavy, blunt, endless. Point it down a corridor they cannot avoid.

spike_pillar.t: A Spike Pillar, my liege. It spits spikes at anything within a tile. Solid, vicious, central.
A spike pillar placed. A blunt little horror — it bites everything that crowds it.
A Spike Pillar. No subtlety, all puncture. The heroes will give it a frightened berth.

dragon_trap.t: A Dragon Trap, my liege — wall-mounted, and it breathes FIRE down its line. Heavy, heavy damage.
A dragon trap placed. The maze just learned to spit flame. They will cook in a corridor.
A Dragon Trap. The nastiest wall in the dungeon. Aim it where they cannot dodge, darling.

spike_pit.t: A Spike Pit, my liege — disguised until they step on it. Heavy damage, and a chance to simply end them.
A spike pit placed. Hidden, patient, occasionally lethal in one go. Tuck it deep.
A Spike Pit. They will not see it until they are in it. The maze's quietest killer.

rotating_blades.t: Rotating Blades, my liege — forever spinning, forever hungry for anything in reach.
A blade trap placed. It does not stop, it does not aim — it simply cuts. Lovely.
Rotating Blades. A wall of whirling teeth. The heroes must thread it or bleed for it.

saw_blade.t: A Saw Blade, my liege — it travels its track endlessly, slicing any hero it overlaps.
A saw blade placed. Walkable track, unwalkable timing. Make them dance, darling.
A Saw Blade. Heavy damage and no collision — it shares the floor and ruins it anyway.

door_lock.t: A Door Lock, my liege — it seals the way until a hero finds the key. A chokepoint with manners.
A door lock placed. It pairs with a Key Chest somewhere — guard that chest, darling.
A Door Lock. Funnel them, frustrate them, make them HUNT for the way through. Delicious.

treasure_chest.t: A Treasure Chest, my liege. Passive gold each day — and a shiny temptation the greedy cannot resist.
A chest placed. It pays you nightly and lures the raid groups. Guard it, or gift it, darling.
A Treasure Chest. Bait that earns its keep. The best lies are the profitable ones.

soul_bound_beacon.t: A Soul-Bound Beacon, my liege — and its Healing Fountain. A safe haven the heroes will trust. Foolishly.
A beacon placed, paired with a fountain — a tempting little oasis. Surround it with teeth.
A Soul-Bound Beacon. It looks like mercy. The dungeon does not do mercy, darling — it does bait.

phylactery_heart.t: The Phylactery Heart, my liege. Your hidden spare life — place it where no hero will think to look.
The Heart is placed. A fourth life, beating in the dark. Guard it like the secret it is.
Your Phylactery Heart. While it beats, death is a mere inconvenience. Hide it WELL, darling.
